title: Loss of Values: Wikipedia as a World Knowledge Poisoning Arena – and in Israel it is particularly painful
text: This week Wikipedia marks 25 years since its founding. It once symbolized the big dream of the Internet: free, collaborative knowledge, accessible to everyone. But in 2026, that dream is crumbling. Wikipedia is no longer just a database, but an ideological battleground, where organized interest groups succeed in distorting, deleting and rewriting historysometimes in the most systematic and deliberate way.

title: Loss of Values: Wikipedia as a World Knowledge Poisoning Arena – and in Israel it is particularly painful
text: It is being replicated and disseminated through AI engines and is rapidly becoming a "knowledge" in the eyes of millions of users around the world, who use it on social networks, schoolwork, and general knowledge. In an age when young people formulate a worldview through social networks and AI motors, disinformation is no longer a theoretical problem: it is a real danger to countries, communities, and public in an age where AI is no longer an issue.

title: Loss of Values: Wikipedia as a World Knowledge Poisoning Arena – and in Israel it is particularly painful
text: Values in English that deal with the State of Israel, Zionism, Jewish history and archaeology in the Land of Israel are being systematically rewritten. Behind the scenes are organized groups promoting a particular narrative, erasing established facts, and blocking editors who try to bring balance, sometimes through votes that lead to the locking of values. This is not a theory. This is a documented reality. It is only recently that Jimmy Wales, a co-founder of Wikipedia, addressed the value of the article as being edited.

Even after the personal intervention of Wales, the biased definition is repeated. Another example is that Israel is the only country to which a dedicated value is being compared to the historical of the Jewish state.

Regardless of whether there be any legitimate or other political criticism of Israel or its government, the solution is not possible.

title: State Learning Committee – and Not a Tribunal Towards
text: The real question following October 7 is not who is to blame, but how a country that knows its enemies, invests billions in its security and possesses advanced intelligence systems fails so deeply. An honest answer to this question cannot be satisfied with the event itself, but requires a look at how the State of Israel learns, defines policy, shapes strategy, and translates knowledge into action in a world that is in constant motion. A state-of-the-art commission of inquiry is not required as a system of war, but rather as a necessary tool for building a safer future.

title: State Learning Committee – and Not a Tribunal Towards
text: Precisely because of this, the fundamental question is not whether we knew, but how we learned, how we interpreted the change, and how we translated an understanding of policy and strategy into practice. Israel operates within a reality that is in rapid and constant motion, global, regional, internal, security, political and technological. In political and military theory, it is a fundamental condition – stability is not a natural condition but rather a product of constant adjustment to changing reality, from the same perception to the given.

title: State Learning Committee – and Not a Tribunal Towards
text: It expresses national interests, values and fundamental preferences, one who is supposed to mediate the changing reality, at least in the security field, to propose relevant modes of action, to challenge the political echelon - these are the levels of execution in the IDF, from the level of the division and above. Policy usually changes at sharp transition points, which is reminiscent of the parable of the frog and the boiling water. In the transition to war, it is likely that the policy is being restructured, and the world is constantly changing.

title: State Learning Committee – and Not a Tribunal Towards
text: The Commission of Inquiry or State or National Learning, is required to examine the "First Israel-Iran War" not only as a collection of separate threats, but as a single multi-stage system, in which Iran, Hezbollah, Hamas, the Houthis, Shiite militias and other elements, were built not only to protect Iran from external threats but in our case, to accept Israel's destruction. Not less. How did we understand, evaluate and act as a state, in the face of this transition, long before October 7?

title: State Learning Committee – and Not a Tribunal Towards
text: Systematic, sequential, and frictional learning are necessary conditions for a better understanding of reality and for identifying a change that requires a re-perception, a re-organization and a totality of actions, policies, security, economic and other consciousness. Systematic learning is a necessary condition for a better understanding of reality and for identifying a change that requires even a re-perception, re-organization and a total of actions, policies, security, economic, and other consciousness.

title: State Learning Committee – and Not a Tribunal Towards
text: A statewide, objective and impartial inquiry committee is bound by reality - but this is not required first and foremost to ascertain facts (this is important) or to locate faults, but to maintain second-rate learning about how the system learns, interprets reality, and makes decisions. The committee should ask: How are learning processes held at the political and security levels? Are there institutional mechanisms for updating perceptions? Is this a real, systematic strategic dialogue, such as in the context of the political system?

title: State Learning Committee – and Not a Tribunal Towards
text: Thus, for example, the policy and strategy that Yitzhak Rabin had in mind were supposed to shape a better future, but in the test of the act, and there are many and varied reasons for this, they failed in a way that challenges us to this day. It is equally possible to say about the policy taken against Hamas in Gaza. The terrible massacre committed by Hamas does not indicate whether the policy according to which it is correct to distinguish between Gaza and the West Bank is simply correct or not.

title: State Learning Committee – and Not a Tribunal Towards
text: The committee is required to examine whether and how intentions were identified alongside capabilities, and whether there was an abilitypolitical and militaryto understand the change and then stop, disrupt or suppress the threat while still in the enemy, and not only to respond after it has erupted. October 7 was not because we did not know, but because the system did not learn in time the meaning of the knowledge. A state commission of inquiry, objective and independent, should first of all be a national learning committee, one that examines the issue, and therefore the strategy and learning processes, to ensure that the State of Israel knows it.

title: State Learning Committee – and Not a Tribunal Towards
text: A state that does not investigate its failures in order to improve, but rather to blame, gives up its ability to change in advance. October 7 presents Israel with a profound test: Should we choose to establish a commission of inquiry that will reflect responsibility, learning and repair, or should we prefer to hang in the past and evade the future? A state commission of inquiry, objective and independent, that will examine policy, strategy and learning processes, is not a threat to the system, is a necessary condition for its reconstruction. Only a country learns that recognizes change and acts in the history of the war.

title: Sovereignty for appearances
text: On May 11, 2022, the political system in Israel went into a state of nervous waiting. It was not a discussion of the Knesset plenum or a cabinet meeting that determined the future of the country, but rather a meeting of the Shura Council of the Islamic Movement. While the Knesset dispersal law was on the agenda and the coalition was leaning on chicken shading, an entire country was waiting for the departure of religious people in a closed room to know whether the government would continue to freeze its membership or bring about the downfall of the government. These moments revealed:

title: Sovereignty for appearances
text: With all the known differences, what we saw then with the Shura Council, we saw again this week with the Council of Torah Sages: the entire country stopped and waited for the decision of a body that no one had chosen, which is not subject to any voter, and whose decisions effectively determine the stability of the coalition and the future of the government. In both cases the issue discussed among those unelected bodies was the boundaries of the sovereignty of the state. In one case, the government's intention to enforce the government's authority is decided.

title: Sovereignty for appearances
text: The fate of the state should be determined in institutions that represent the public in all its shades and are transparent to its criticism. When closed councils, which do not bear responsibility towards all citizens, are those that decide what the boundaries of sovereignty are of the state institutions within the State of Israel, we are witnessing a weakening of sovereignty. This is true for the Supreme Court, but also for the Shura Council and the Council of Torah Sages, it is not only for the establishment of the political agendas, but for the establishment of these bodies.

title: Sovereignty for appearances
text: When a small group, elected by a handful of people or by internal leadership, decides the fate of millions of citizens, this is no less a serious threat. Will we continue to talk about the will of the people while decisions of existential weight are made outside the elected institutions? Or will we take responsibility and correct the rules so that the power is truly restored to the right-of-choice people? Oz Ben Nun is the CEO of the Revo Center – the ideological home of the Servant Alliance.

title: On Trojan Horses
text: If an alien had landed in Israel in 2018, it would have been difficult to explain why Israel was asking an "absolutely complex" enemy state to transfer millions of dollars in cash to an organization that seeks to destroy it. But there is no need to resort to aliens in real time, as already in real time there has been harsh criticism from the Shin Bet and the security system that warned that some of the Qatari money being funneled into the Gaza Strip was flowing into Hamas's military wing instead of reaching a civilian population.

title: On Trojan Horses
text: The stated logic of the policy was to buy temporary quiet, separate the arenas (Gaza and the West Bank), and manage the conflict instead of solving it. However, critics of the money transfer policy argued already in real time that Hamas' economic and institutional strengthening would deepen its control of the Gaza Strip and increase its defensive and offensive military capabilities. The alternatives to this policy also made it clear that it was the stopping of the money, changing the rules of the game, or a broader political move.

title: On Trojan Horses
text: However, a special type of error is one of fundamentally wrong policies, accepted by leaders who act against their own interests, even though they are aware of the criticisms hurled at them and their actions in real time, such as the policy that led to 7/10. Such was the error of the people of Troy. Thousands of years ago, the Trojans stood in a ten-year siege of their city, during which the Greeks repeatedly tried to break through the walls of the fortified city without success.

title: On Trojan Horses
text: Since then, the Trojan horse has been a symbol of cunning and deception. However, the introduction of the horse has not only been an act of Greek subterfuge, but an folly of the people of Troy and is an example of a leadership that has acted in complete opposition to its own interests. As in the case of the historical money, the Trojans have been known to have made mistakes.

title: On Trojan Horses
text: The source of folly is not ignorance, but a combination of arrogance, institutional inertia, short-term political interests, and a refusal to recognize a changing reality. In Troy, as in the transfer of Qatari dollars to Hamas, these parameters were met: the most destructive of the situation in Israel’s history, the destruction of the horse in the city, and the change of the dollars in Hamas.

title: On Trojan Horses
text: Despite all the indications received, voices warning of a 1973 impending war were classified as excessive, pessimistic and unrealistic, and the enemy's preparedness was explained as an exercise or political move. The belief in the depth of Israeli deterrence, coupled with the desire to preserve strategic stability and avoid escalation, led to a lenient interpretation of the information and to the suppression of warnings that did not correspond to the "imperiality" of political considerations.

title: On Trojan Horses
text: The refusal of the main opposition parties to act together causes visible damage in real time. Instead of uniting and creating a large and self-assured political system that will lead to the realization of their stated goal, replacing the 7/10th government, the opposition leaders contribute to the establishment of the consciousness that the Likud is the only dominant party. This perception affects not only voters in Israel but also decision makers in the international arena, led by the American president who, in the absence of a realistic alternative to Netanyahu, continues to hold on him.

title: On Trojan Horses
text: The collective cost to this conduct is not theoretical but tangible: an impairment of the ability to establish a credible governmental alternative and to deepen the sense of public helplessness. Thus, the schism in the liberal-democratic camp becomes not a tactical error, but a consistent pattern of political folly that could result in a strategic-scale loss. If the opposition leaders do not seek to be remembered as a "michalite opponent of the 2026 elections," as having by their own hands paved the way of losing because of egoistic considerations, he is now too late.

title: Is the Iranian regime facing a fall?
text: The current wave of protests in Iran is redeploying the question of the stability of the Islamic regime, but this time under severe and fundamentally different conditions than those that characterized the previous waves of protest. A rare combination of economic collapse, deep infrastructure crises, a change of consciousness in the public and increasing international pressure creates an explosive reality, in which the very existence of the regime is not as self-evident as it was in the past. The basis of the protest is primarily existential-economic shock.

title: Is the Iranian regime facing a fall?
text: In contrast to previous protests focused on political rights or the cost of living, this time the public asks a much more fundamental question: Is the state capable of providing water, electricity, and a stable currency? At the same time, a profound change of consciousness is taking place. The Iranian population is young, urban, and less ideologically committed to the Islamic revolution. This protest is still a strengthening of the regime's international policy, but it is also expressing widespread alienation.

title: Is the Iranian regime facing a fall?
text: Cautious optimism in the face of a changing reality despite the barriers, the cumulative conditions make the regime more vulnerable than ever before. External intervention, the people's stand-in behind Reza Pelleway, the expansion of the demonstrations and the reversal of the reeds in the security apparatuses may turn the protest into a historic turning point. Amir Avivi is the chairman of the security forces.

title: Levin and Rotman adopted Barak's interpretive concept

Reuven says to Shimon: I propose to sell you a horse that I own at a certain price. Shimon replies: I agree to buy the horse at the same price. Supposedly a contract is clear. title: Levin and Rotman adopted Barak's interpretive concept

title: What will happen in a state without rulers when everything is determined by artificial intelligence?
text: There is something tempting about the idea of a leader who doesn't lie. Especially for those who are tired of figuring out who is working on him and how. A leader who doesn't get involved in corruption scandals, and who doesn't promise mountains and hills before the elections just to disappear right after the polls close. Despair in the Balkans Government of Albania, not exactly the first democratic lighthouse that comes to mind, thought exactly of it when she decided to appoint an AI-based "s" who will oversee public tenders. It doesn't. It doesn't come from a tech lover.

title: What will happen in a state without rulers when everything is determined by artificial intelligence?
text: Instead of 120 chosen people fighting for our attention on Twitter, sitting one big, cool, quietly buzzing server that offers an artificially enhanced democracy for example, which decides all the most important decisions about our lives according to the data we fed it. It is no longer a question of science fiction or an episode of "Black Mirror". While we are still stuck arguing about the reform or about who will sit on the Committee for the Selection of Judges, this reality is rushing to bring forth an artificial intelligence to us. Let us go with this fantasy to the end.

title: What will happen in a state without rulers when everything is determined by artificial intelligence?
text: On paper, this sounds like the ultimate fantasy: instead of going to the polls once every four years (or five months) and choosing someone to forget you a second time, you will get a personal bot. He will get to know you better than your mother. He will know what you think about public transportation on Saturday, the security situation, housing prices. About religion, security and state. He will know everything about you. He will be you. This is basically the "poorous apple" of the new era: he looks perfect, and that is the problem.

title: What will happen in a state without rulers when everything is determined by artificial intelligence?
text: After all, democracy is based on our ability to curse the situation, to demonstrate, and to feel that we have the power to drive out those who have screwed up in the next election. What kind of a regime will be created here? So democracy is not, nor is theocracy. Although from the citizen side, imagination is quite disturbing. But here the difference is in the source of authority, in the replacement of the divine mystery with the algorithm. In both cases, the decision comes from a place where we are unable to reason.

title: What will happen in a state without rulers when everything is determined by artificial intelligence?
text: For this moment may sound like the libertarian dream: a world without rulers, without politicians, a free market of ideas run by the "invisible hand" of technology. But anarchy means a lack of authority. Here we get the exact opposite: absolute, obsessive authority, which is everywhere. In anarchy there is chaos; here there is an exemplary, defiant order. title: What will happen in a state without rulers when everything is determined by artificial intelligence?
text: The quiet will be exemplary, because it is impossible to dispute a code that no one understands. The machine will be blind to concepts like compassion or social justice; it will see only maximizing profits and operational efficiency. In such a reality, we are no longer citizens with rights, but lines of code in an operating system that cannot be extinguished. Look at our Knesset, with all the disgust that sometimes floats there. Two MKs are screaming at each other's perilous path. We may seem ugly, may not even effective, but it is human.

title: What will happen in a state without rulers when everything is determined by artificial intelligence?
text: To use AI not to replace us, but to help us become better citizens. To use technology to expose corruption, not to hide it behind an algorithm. Because if we give up our right to make a mistake and decide, we will one day find out that we have built new gods for ourselves, and that we have become little screws in the machine. Keren Shachar is a guide to using artificial intelligence to create a new world.
